other_observers: Nathan Pieplow, Joey Kellner, Joe Roller, Allison Hilf, Norm Erthal, Chris Owens, Lisa Edwards, Paul Differding, and others; first found by me, and identified by Nathan Pieplow and myself
description: the bird was a medium sized warbler, about the size of nearby Townsend's Warblers, though appeared to be a bit heavier in build. The bill was black, and appeared to be typical of Dendroica. The legs were black.  The crown and face were a yellowish olive-green. The bird had a paler area behind the auricular, and along the eyebrow, and as a crescent under the eye, making the auricular are stand out a bit. The back was the same yellowish olive-green color. The wings were bluish-gray, with two bold white wingbars. The rump was the same color as the back. The tail was grayish, with large white tail corners.  The throat and chest were bright yellow, with some fairly faint olive-colored streaks about the same color as the upperparts on the sides of the chest. These streaks were hard to see from a distance, but fairly evident at close range. The belly and undertail coverts were white.Behaviors: the bird was foraging at mid to upper levels in the trees, associated with a large flock of Wilson's, Yellow, and Townsend's Warblers. It could be hard to keep track of.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
